elements.title: Tom Scutt
elements.contents: ... that a Tony Award–winning set by Tom Scutt (pictured) has the audience entering through a side entrance to make a "descent into the cabaret"?

elements.title: Wyoming Supreme Court censured a judge
elements.contents: ... that the Wyoming Supreme Court censured a judge for announcing that she would not marry same-sex couples?

elements.title: pretext for war
elements.contents: ... that the image of Christ on gold coins was a pretext for war?

elements.title: Syed Abid Husain
elements.contents: ... that Syed Abid Husain's play The Veil of Ignorance explores social discourse on access to modern education by Indian women?

elements.title: her third album
elements.contents: ... that Mary Lattimore recorded her third album in a barn?

elements.title: Aaron Mahnke
elements.contents: ... that Lore podcaster Aaron Mahnke thought to make the show after his history and folklore PDFs made for fans of his thriller novels became too large to maintain?

elements.title: government officials
elements.contents: ... that during the Ming dynasty, prospective government officials needed to write an eight-legged essay about the Four Books in Confucianism for their examinations?

elements.title: a New York State TV station
elements.contents: ... that the first studios of a New York State TV station had floors that were so uneven that a camera could wobble as it moved?

elements.title: the 1990s Wired style guide
elements.contents: ... that the 1990s Wired style guide defines WTFIGO and userid, but not WTF or username?
